growth_records.medium: Anoxic basal mineral medium + 10 mM monomethylamine (MMA)

up.method: provided as substrate; conversion tracked by 13C/D3 stable isotope tracing (2 mM of the 10 mM was labeled)

sec.units: uM produced in 34 days (6th transfer); ~116 uM/day volumetric
sec.method: headspace GC over long-term cultivation; not biomass-normalized

sec.units: uM by Day 38 = 35.9% conversion of the 2 mM labeled MMA fraction (356 uM = 17.8% by Day 32)
uM by Day 38 = 10.65% of 2 mM labeled MMA (138.71 uM = 6.9% by Day 32)
uM by Day 38 = 19.7% of 2 mM labeled MMA fraction

growth_records.cite: Mackenzie M. Lynes et al. Horizontal gene transfer constrains the timing of methanogen evolution. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae026. [PMC10945360]
growth_records.snippet: the culture produced 3943 uM methane within 34 days ... On average across six replicates, the culture converted 13CH3-NH2 to 356 uM 13CH4 (17.8%) and 138.71 uM 13CO2 (6.9%) by Day 32 ... to 717.7 uM 13CH4 (35.9%) and 212.95 uM 13CO2 (10.65%
growth_records.notes: Enrichment (mixed) culture, NOT axenic; Ca. M. hypatiae dominant (67% cells by FISH; 92.8% rel. abund. in SIT). Methylotrophic methanogenesis: MMA -> CH4 (+ some CO2 via oxidative branch of Wood-Ljungdahl). Rates are VOLUMETRIC (uM per culture volume over days), NOT per gDW, so no qS/qP in mmol/gDW/h can be derived (no biomass/OD given). Volumetric CH4 rate estimate: 3943 uM / 34 d = 116 uM CH4 d^-1 (6th transfer). Isotope-conversion percentages are relative to the 2 mM labeled fraction of the 10 mM MMA (356/2000=17.8%; 717.7/2000=35.9%; 138.71/2000=6.9%; 212.95/2000=10.65%; 394.76/2000=19.7%) - arithmetic matches paper. Optimal growth 64-70 deg C, pH 7.8; incubated at 70 deg C. No methane with BES inhibitor or without MMA (negative controls). No explicit specific growth rate or doubling time reported; exponential phase noted at Day 32. A related enrichment Ca. M. nevadensis (cited ref 35, Great Boiling Spring NV) is noted to yield up to 158 uM methane after 2 weeks at 75 deg C - that value is FROM another study, not this paper.
